zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Phelipp於大約1310年出生在英國賀特福爾巴爾多可時，她的父親Thomas Phelipp27歲，母親Isabella de Chelmsford21歲。她於1330年與John Darset在英國巴金哈姆爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1389年10月11日之後在英國艾可莎佛朗 瓦爾登。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Phelipp于大约1310年出生在英国賀特福爾巴爾多可时，她的父亲Thomas Phelipp27岁，母亲Isabella de Chelmsford21岁。她于1330年与John Darset在英国巴金哈姆爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1389年10月11日之后在英国艾可莎佛朗 瓦爾登。

nameMeanings.name: Phelipp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Hans, Heinz, Manfred, Dieter, Erhard, Erhardt, Guenther, Gunther, Helmut, Hilde, Inge.
German and Jewish (western Ashkenazic): from the personal name Philipp (see Philip ).
Scottish (Glamorgan) and English (southeastern): variant of   Philip  .

variants.name: Philip
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Varughese, Mathai, Babu, Reji, Anil, Binu, Neena, Prema, Raju, Reena, Susheela, Aleykutty.

Scottish, English, German, Dutch, and Jewish: from the Greek personal name Philippos (from philein ‘to love’ + hippos ‘horse’). In the New Testament this name is borne by one of the apostles; it was also borne by various other early Christian saints. It owes part of its popularity to the medieval romances about Alexander the Great, whose father was Philip of Macedon. As a Jewish name, it represents a borrowing of the personal name from Christians. As a Highland Scottish surname, it represents an Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Fhilib ‘son of Philip’. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Spanish Felipe , Catalan Felip, Polish, Czech, Slovak, Slovenian or Croatian Filip , Albanian Filipi , and their derivatives (see examples at Philips ). The name Philip is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Philipose and Pothen ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Phillip .
French (southern) and Breton (Finistère): variant of   Philippe  .

variants.name: Philippi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Florian, Hans, Klaus, Kurt, Udo.

German, Dutch, Flemish, and French (mainly Alsace and Lorraine): Latinized (humanistic) patronymic from the personal name Philipp (German), Philip (Dutch), or Philippe (French), using the genitive of its Latin form Philippus. In southern France and Corsica this is a surname of Italian origin (compare 2 below).

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Phelipp was born about 1310, in Baldock, Hertfordshire, England, her father, Thomas Phelipp, was 27 and her mother, Isabella de Chelmsford, was 21. She married John Darset in 1330, in Buckinghamshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died after 11 October 1389, in Saffron Walden, Essex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Phelipp est née environ 1310, à Baldock, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Phelipp, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Isabella de Chelmsford, avait 21 ans. Elle a épousé John Darset en 1330, à Buckinghamshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée après 11 octobre 1389, à Saffron Walden, Essex, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Phelipp um 1310 in Baldock, Hertfordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Thomas Phelipp 27 und ihre Mutter Isabella de Chelmsford 21. Sie hat 1330 in Buckinghamshire, England John Darset geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb nach 11. Oktober 1389 in Saffron Walden, Essex, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Phelipp è nata il circa 1310 nel Baldock, Hertfordshire, England, sua padre, Thomas Phelipp, aveva 27 anni e sua madre, Isabella de Chelmsford, aveva 21 anni. Lei sposato John Darset nel 1330, nel Buckinghamshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì dopo il 11 ottobre 1389, nel Saffron Walden, Essex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Phelippが約1310年にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアボードックで生まれた時，父Thomas Phelippは27歳，母Isabella de Chelmsfordは21歳であった。1330年にイギリスブクインガムシュアイアでJohn Darsetと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1389年10月11日以後にイギリスエセックスサフロン ワルデンで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Phelipp(이)가 대략 1310년 영국 허트퍼드실르 볼독에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Phelipp(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabella de Chelmsford(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1330년 영국 벅잉함실르에서 John Darset(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1389년 10월 11일 이후 영국 에세스 사프런 월든에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Phelipp nasceu aproximadamente 1310, em Baldock, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Phelipp, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Isabella de Chelmsford, tinha 21 anos. Ela casou-se com John Darset em 1330, em Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ela faleceu após 11 de outubro de 1389, em Saffron Walden, Essex, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Phelipp родилась около 1310 г. в местности балдокк, хиртфордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Thomas Phelipp было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabella de Chelmsford, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Darset в 1330 г. в местности буккингхамшир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла после 11 октябрь 1389 г. в местности саффрон валден, эссекс, Англия.
